sanskrit: प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि। विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान्॥
transliteration: prakṛtiṃ puruṣaṃ caiva viddhy anādī ubhāv api | vikārāṃś ca guṇāṃś caiva viddhi prakṛti-sambhavān ||
literal_meaning: Know both prakṛti (material nature) and puruṣa (conscious being) to be beginningless; know transformations and the three guṇas to be born of prakṛti.
contextual_meaning: The mind-body system and the observing self are co-eternal; all moods, traits, and psychological fluctuations are emergent properties of the mind-body matrix, not of the true Self.

shadow: fear of being reducible to biology

psychological_state: Meta-cognitive insight—recognizing that anxious or depressive states are products of the interaction between body and mind, not definitions of identity.
trigger_scenario: A client realizes, 'My panic is just a wave in my nervous system, not who I am,' and feels the first taste of distance from the symptom.
anchor_text: You are the silent witness, not the ever-changing mind.
